behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: failure is in local template-lint logic, not a cross-module runtime handoff. `REAL-INFRA`: no OS/network/subprocess is required. `LLM-DEP`: no model judgment is involved. Rejected Integration/System/Agentic because behavior text explicitly says `static prompt-template lint check`, which disqualifies higher-boundary needs.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function on prompt-template strings (including leaked SKILL.md phase text fixtures) and assert it rejects leakage patterns and accepts clean templates.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: this failure appears when template interpolation + fixture wiring + assembly interact; a single-function test is too low. `REAL-INFRA`: can run in-process without real network/subprocess. `LLM-DEP`: no dependence on model output quality. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior focuses on assembled prompt content before model execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert the final prompt text has no leaked phase instructions.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: proving behavior is caused by loaded skill context (not hard-coded prompt rules) requires end-to-end invocation across loader + prompt builder + model response check. `LLM-DEP`: correctness depends on what the real model does with that context; a stub would hide this tautology failure mode. Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one invocation/decision path, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real test invocation that reads actual plugin/skill files, send a user-style prompt to a live model, and assert behavior reflects skill-context effects rather than explicit hard-coded phase instructions in prompt text.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: failure is in harness/module wiring for variant selection and file-based skill loading. `REAL-INFRA`: local filesystem interaction is enough; no real network/subprocess requirement. `LLM-DEP`: this behavior checks prompt invariance and variant switching capability, not model output quality. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about running setup (`can run ... without changing test prompt text`) rather than judging output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real harness to execute two runs (baseline skill and degraded/missing SKILL.md) with identical user prompt text and assert only skill-source inputs differ.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: `LLM-DEP`: pass/fail depends on model behavior changing when skill content regresses; deterministic stubs can mask this. `MOCK-HIDE`: mocked outputs could still satisfy checks and miss real regression. Rejected Workflow because this is a single behavioral outcome under one prompt, not multi-step agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run baseline vs degraded SKILL.md with identical user-style prompt against a live model and assert the degraded variant causes the behavioral test to fail while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: ordering bug appears at boundary between model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser. `REAL-INFRA`: real external infra is not required; in-process fake model response can exercise ordering. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets capture sequencing (`before any JSON parsing`) rather than correctness of model judgments.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the response-processing pipeline with a controlled model-response payload and assert the raw artifact is persisted first, then parsing/extraction executes.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: `MOCK-MISS`: this is about assertion layer behavior integrated with structured output handling, not just one pure helper in isolation. `LLM-DEP`: structural-check logic itself can be validated with fixtures; no real model needed for this behavior alone. Rejected Agentic because behavior text emphasizes assertion design (`enforce structural constraints`) rather than live model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real assertion module against crafted outputs where keywords are present but structure is wrong, and assert structural checks fail while keyword-only checks would pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says `under live model execution`; correctness depends on real model outputs where regressions can appear despite keyword presence. `MOCK-HIDE`: fixed mock outputs can hide these misses. Rejected Workflow because this describes one model output evaluation step, not a chained multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the behavioral test against a live model and compare outcomes of structural assertions vs keyword-only checks, verifying at least one live regression is caught only by structural assertions.
